Hi Sir/Madam,
Hope you are doing well! I am having some troubles in SSHing into the HPC.
I can successfully log into [https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk](https://login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk/) using my CRSid (vd327), and my HPC account appears to be active.
However, when I try to log in via SSH (e.g. ssh vd327@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk), I am prompted for my password and then a TOTP verification code, but after three attempts I receive “Permission denied”.
I have verified that:
- I am using my Cambridge Raven/UIS password
- MFA/TOTP is set up and working for login-web
- The SSH host key has been accepted correctly
Could you please advise me on what I should do/ how to fix this?
Many thanks,
Vivek Dherani
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Authentication / SSH / MFA (TOTP)
Technical Action Required: No (user configuration issue)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (access blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User can successfully log into login-web.hpc.cam.ac.uk but receives “Permission denied” when attempting SSH login to login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk.

User reports:
- Raven/UIS password is correct.
- MFA works for login-web.
- SSH host key accepted.
- Password recently changed.

Web login MFA and SSH MFA are separate enrolments. A common cause of this behaviour is that MFA has not yet been enrolled for SSH access (multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk), even though web MFA works.

ACTION

1) Explain SSH MFA requirement
   - Clarify that SSH requires a separate MFA enrolment from login-web.

2) Provide documentation
   https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html#walkthrough-ssh-to-multi-hpc-cam-ac-uk

3) Instruct user to enrol SSH MFA via:

   ssh <CRSid>@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk

   They should:
   - Enter UIS/Raven password.
   - Scan the displayed QR code into their authenticator app.
   - Confirm a new token is created (label similar to “CSD3:SSH Login”).

4) After enrolment, retry SSH to:

   ssh <CRSid>@login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk

5) If this does not resolve the issue:
   - Ask the user to paste the full SSH output (including prompts but excluding any codes).
   - Escalate only if MFA enrolment succeeds but authentication still fails.

EXPECTED OUTCOME
Once SSH MFA is enrolled correctly, authentication via SSH should succeed using:
- UIS/Raven password
- TOTP from the new “CSD3:SSH Login” token

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Vivek,

Although your MFA is working for login-web, SSH access requires MFA to be set up separately.

A common cause of this behaviour is that SSH MFA has not yet been enrolled. Please follow the walkthrough here:

https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/mfa.html#walkthrough-ssh-to-multi-hpc-cam-ac-uk

You can begin the enrolment process by running:

  ssh <CRSid>@multi.hpc.cam.ac.uk

You will be prompted for your UIS/Raven password and then shown a QR code to scan into your authenticator app.

user_input:
Hi,
I can’t submit any Slurm jobs using the Callender+ account, PI Dr Callender copied in,  because it doesn’t seem to have any partitions attached to it, so every sbatch call fails with an “Invalid account or account/partition combination” error. I can install packages on the login node, but can’t see that my environment works end-to-end without running jobs on compute nodes. I would be grateful for your advice on whether a CPU partition is available under callender+ and how to resolve this please?
Best wishes,
Megha
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / Account & Partition Configuration
Technical Action Required: No (user-side correction)
Escalation Required: No (unless account not present in mybalance)
Urgency: Medium (job submission blocked)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports:

  sbatch: error: Batch job submission failed:
  Invalid account or account/partition combination specified

They attempted to submit using:
  #SBATCH --account=callender+
  #SBATCH --partition=cclake

This error typically indicates one of the following:

1) The Slurm account name is incorrect (account names are case-sensitive).
2) The account name does not exactly match the allocation shown in Slurm.
3) The account/partition combination is not valid for the user.
4) The user is not using the correct Slurm account identifier format.

The correct way to determine valid accounts is via the `mybalance` command.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Ask the user to confirm available accounts
   Instruct them to run:

     mybalance

   This will display the exact account names and usage limits.

2) Confirm account string matches exactly
   - Account names are case-sensitive.
   - Hyphens and underscores are distinct.
   - Plus signs (“+”) are not normally used in Slurm account names.

3) Ensure correct SBATCH syntax
   In the submission script:

     #SBATCH --account=<EXACT_ACCOUNT_NAME_FROM_MYBALANCE>
     #SBATCH --partition=<VALID_PARTITION_FOR_ACCOUNT>

4) Validate partition compatibility
   If unsure, ask the user to provide:

     mybalance
     sinfo -s

   Confirm that:
   - The account is listed.
   - The partition is valid for that account.
   - The user is a member of that Slurm account.

5) If the account does not appear in `mybalance`
   - Verify account membership.
   - Escalate to cluster administration if necessary.

COMMON CAUSES TO CHECK

- Typographical error in account name.
- Using an informal label (e.g. project nickname) instead of the Slurm account identifier.
- Case mismatch.
- Replacing hyphens with underscores.
- Using outdated account name.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Unable to submit Slurm jobs on callender+

Hello Megha,

The error you’re seeing usually indicates that the account name or account/partition combination is not valid.

Could you please run:

  mybalance

This will show the exact Slurm account names available to you. Account names are case-sensitive and must match exactly in your submission script.

Once you have confirmed the correct account name, update your script to include:

  #SBATCH --account=<exact account name>
  #SBATCH --partition=<valid partition>

If you’re still seeing the error after confirming the account name from `mybalance`, please send the output of `mybalance` and your updated submission script so we can take a closer look.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume account misconfiguration without verifying `mybalance`.
- Do not manually alter Slurm accounts unless membership is confirmed missing.
- Avoid guessing account names; always use system-reported identifiers.

I also investigated the problem by trying to start interactive SL3 CPU sessions using this command: 
sintr -A WOODLAND-SL3-CPU -p cclake -N1 -n1 -t 1:0:0 --qos=INTR
, and the same error message occurred. I tried cclake, icelake, skylake for -p option, but the same error occurred repeatedly. 
 
So could you please help me find out what was going wrong with my SL3 CPU submission? Thank you very much and I am looking forward to your reply. 
 
Best wishes,

Hello HPC support team,
We'd like to add Cambridge MPhil students Boqiao Zhang (bz317) and Oszkar Urban (ou222) to:
1) RDS disk
`rds-posecraft-5SVx1x5hoTY`
2) My supervisor Prof Cengiz Oztireli's accounts
OZTIRELI-SL2-CPU
OZTIRELI-SL2-GPU
OZTIRELI-SL3-CPU
OZTIRELI-SL3-GPU
Please let us know if anything or any approval is needed. Many thanks!
Best wishes,
Zhilin
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage Membership / Slurm Account Access
Technical Action Required: Yes (conditional – Slurm account membership)
Portal Action Required: Yes (RDS membership via Self Service)
Escalation Required: Conditional (PI approval required for Slurm accounts)
Urgency: Low–Medium (collaborator onboarding)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests:

1) Add two students to an RDS storage project:
   rds-posecraft-5SVx1x5hoTY

2) Add the same users to multiple Slurm accounts:
   OZTIRELI-SL2-CPU
   OZTIRELI-SL2-GPU
   OZTIRELI-SL3-CPU
   OZTIRELI-SL3-GPU

Key principles:

- RDS project membership is managed by the Data Owner or Data Manager via the Self Service portal. HPC Support does not directly administer storage memberships.
- Slurm account access can only be granted with explicit approval from the PI/account owner.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) RDS Membership
   - Inform user that storage project membership is controlled via the Self Service portal.
   - Direct them to confirm that the Data Owner or Data Manager has added the students.
   - If necessary, advise them to check:
       https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/

2) Slurm Account Access
   - Confirm whether explicit approval from the account owner (PI) has been provided.
   - If not, request written approval from the PI before proceeding.
   - Once approval is received, add the users to the specified Slurm accounts.

3) Do NOT:
   - Add users to Slurm accounts without PI approval.
   - Modify storage memberships manually.
   - Assume requester has authority unless they are confirmed Data Owner/PI.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (IF APPROVAL NOT CLEAR)

- Are you the Data Owner or Data Manager of the RDS project?
- Can the PI/account owner confirm approval for adding these users to the Slurm accounts?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Adding collaborators to RDS and Slurm accounts

Dear Zhilin,

Regarding the RDS project, storage memberships are managed by the Data Owner or Data Manager via the Self Service portal. We do not directly administer storage project memberships. Please ensure the relevant Data Owner/Data Manager has added the students through:

  https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/

For the Slurm accounts, we can add the students once we have explicit approval from the account owner (PI). If this has not yet been provided, could the PI please confirm approval?

Once confirmed, we will proceed accordingly.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Storage access must follow Data Owner governance.
- Slurm account additions require PI approval.
- Do not bypass project ownership controls.
- Maintain auditability of account access changes.

- User is external (UKAEA email).
- Invitation link has an expiry date.
- “Local Keycloak entry” indicates use of the documented “without MyAccessID (last resort)” workflow.
- Account must be created in Keycloak before the user can accept the AIRR invitation via “Sign in with Keycloak”.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm invitation context (internal)
   - Verify invitation exists for the user email.
   - Confirm project name: “UKAEA Early Adopters”.
   - Confirm partition/access level requested.

2) Escalate to AIRR team (if not directly handled by HPC Support)
   - Forward ticket to AIRR administrators.
   - Include:
       • Full name
       • Organisation
       • Email
       • Project/partition name
   - Mark urgency if invitation expiry is near.

3) Create local Keycloak account (AIRR/admin action)
   - Create account using provided email.
   - Trigger password setup email.
   - Ensure account is linked to the appropriate realm.

4) Notify user once action is underway
   - Explain that once the local account has been created, they will receive a password setup email.
   - Advise checking spam/junk folders.
   - Instruct them to:
       a) Set password,
       b) Visit invitation link,
       c) Choose “Sign in with Keycloak”,
       d) Use newly created credentials.

5) Provide documentation link
   - Reference official guidance for “Accessing without MyAccessID (last resort)”.

6) Do NOT:
   - Manually approve the project membership unless that is part of the AIRR workflow.
   - Ask for passwords.
   - Override the invitation process.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Has the invitation expired?
- Have you received the password setup email?
- Are you able to access the invitation link successfully?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: UKAEA Early Adopters – AIRR Access

Hi Waqar,

We will arrange creation of the local Keycloak account needed for this AIRR invitation.

Once the account has been created, you should receive an email to set your password. After that, please visit the invitation link, select “Sign in with Keycloak”, and log in using those credentials.

For reference, please see:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/pvc.html#accessing-without-myaccessid-last-resort

Please let us know if you encounter any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not transmit passwords via email.
- Do not request sensitive credentials.
- Ensure external accounts are created only through the approved Keycloak workflow.
- Respect invitation expiry deadlines but do not bypass approval controls.

user_input:
Hello,
I am trying to submit jobs to run my analysis using my GPU allocation for my project.
It seems that there is a 36 hour wall time limit and max number of jobs running at the same time.
Currently I am setting of my analysis with:
#SBATCH --array=0-99
#SBATCH --time=35:30:00
So I don’t think I can make max use of my allocation. Are you able to advice what I can do for this?
Also can you advice what settings to use in the slurm script if I do not need a GPU?
Many thanks
Sam
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / GPU Scheduling / Walltime Limits
Technical Action Required: No (policy explanation + workflow guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (jobs being cancelled due to time limit)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is concerned that GPU jobs cannot exceed the 36-hour walltime limit and asks how to make best use of the allocation.

Key principles:
- GPU partitions enforce a maximum walltime limit (e.g. 36 hours).
- This is a scheduler policy limit, not a configuration error.
- The limit applies regardless of remaining budgeted GPU hours.
- Allocated GPU hours (account balance) are separate from maximum walltime per job.
- Long-running workflows should use checkpointing and job chaining.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Confirm policy limit
   - Explain that the 36-hour walltime is an enforced scheduler limit.
   - It cannot be overridden per job at the user level.

2) Clarify allocation model
   - GPU hour budget defines total consumption allowed.
   - Walltime limit defines maximum duration of a single job.
   - Having remaining hours does not permit exceeding the per-job limit.

3) Recommend checkpointing (best practice)
   - Modify code to periodically write restart/checkpoint files.
   - Submit jobs so that they resume from the last checkpoint.
   - Use job arrays or chained submissions to continue computation.

4) Optional: suggest job chaining
   - Use dependent jobs:
       sbatch --dependency=afterok:<jobid> next_job.sh
   - Or include automatic resubmission logic if checkpoint detected.

6) Do NOT:
   - Promise walltime extensions.
   - Modify partition policy.
   - Suggest splitting allocation across multiple concurrent long jobs to bypass limits.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (IF NEEDED)
- Which partition/account are you using?
- How long does your full workflow need to run?
- Does your code support checkpoint/restart?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Support for job submissions

Hi Sam,

The 36-hour walltime limit is a scheduler policy on the GPU partition and cannot be extended for individual jobs. Even if you have sufficient GPU hours remaining in your allocation, the maximum runtime per job is still capped.

To run longer workflows, we recommend implementing checkpointing so that your job can resume from the last saved state once the walltime is reached. You can then submit follow-up jobs to continue from the checkpoint.

Please let us know if you would like guidance on setting up checkpointing or job dependencies.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Walltime limits are enforced at scheduler level.
- Allocation balance does not override per-job limits.
- Avoid suggesting policy circumvention.
- Promote checkpoint/restart as standard HPC best practice.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Job Submission / Resource Allocation [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is trying to submit jobs to run their analysis using their GPU allocation, but is limited by the 36 hour wall time limit and the maximum number of jobs running at the same time [1]. The user is currently using the `#SBATCH --array=0-99` and `#SBATCH --time=35:30:00` directives in their submission script [1].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To make maximum use of the allocation, the user can use array jobs with a limit on the number of concurrent jobs, for example `#SBATCH --array=0-99%32` [1]. This will allow up to 32 jobs to run concurrently, and the next job will start only after one of the previous 32 has finished [1]. If the user needs to run more than 100 tasks, they can split them into multiple jobs [1]. For jobs that do not require a GPU, the user can use a different submission script without the `--gres=gpu` directive [2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more information about your workflow and the number of tasks you need to run?
- Are you using a specific GPU type or architecture?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To make maximum use of your GPU allocation, you can use array jobs with a limit on the number of concurrent jobs, for example `#SBATCH --array=0-99%32`. This will allow up to 32 jobs to run concurrently, and the next job will start only after one of the previous 32 has finished. If you need to run more than 100 tasks, you can split them into multiple jobs. For jobs that do not require a GPU, you can use a different submission script without the `--gres=gpu` directive.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- The 36 hour wall time limit and the maximum number of jobs running at the same time are policy limits that cannot be exceeded [1][2].
- The user should ensure that their submission script is correctly configured to avoid exceeding these limits [1][2].

Namu

</MESSAGE><MESSAGE id=0003 role=HELPDESK_ASSIGNEE>
Dear Namu,
One way to do this is with array jobs. If you add this line:
```
#SBATCH --array=0-x%32
```
at the end of the SBATCH directives on your submission script then you script will execute x number of tasks but only 32 tasks will run in parallel at any time. This way you can have the maximum of 32 jobs running and the next job will start only after any of the previous 32 has finished. If you have many (thousands) jobs to run (your x) you can split it in multiple jobs. For example you can do #SBATCH --array=0-500%32 if you want to run 500 tasks in total or #SBATCH --array=0-1000%32 if you want to run 1000 tasks.
Let me know if you have any other questions.
Kind regards,
Avraam

user_input:
Hi,
Just a quick Q please - if I running a process on Wilkes using 1 node and 1 GPU and 1 task but I want to increase the number of CPUs used from 32 to 76, how would I do that?
I only need 1 GPU, but I would like to use 76 CPUs, can I increase the task number to achieve this?
Thank you for the information.
Cheers,
Mel
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Slurm / GPU-CPU Resource Ratios / Wilkes3 (Ampere)
Technical Action Required: No (scheduler policy clarification)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is running on Wilkes3 (Ampere) requesting:
- 1 node
- 1 GPU
- 1 task
- Wants to increase CPUs from 32 to 76 while still using only 1 GPU.

On Wilkes3 Ampere nodes, Slurm enforces a CPU-per-GPU ratio:
- Maximum 32 CPUs per GPU.
- This is a scheduler-level constraint.
- Users cannot request arbitrary CPU counts independent of GPU count.

Therefore:
- 1 GPU → maximum 32 CPUs.
- To request ~76 CPUs, the user must request 3 GPUs (32 × 3 = 96 CPUs maximum available).

The scheduler will not allow 76 CPUs with only 1 GPU.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Explain enforced policy
   - Ampere nodes allocate CPUs in proportion to GPUs.
   - 32 CPUs per GPU maximum.
   - This prevents GPU starvation and ensures fair resource use.

2) Calculate requirement
   - 76 CPUs ÷ 32 CPUs per GPU ≈ 2.38 GPUs.
   - Since GPUs are indivisible, user must request 3 GPUs.

3) Provide correct submission guidance
   Example directives:
     #SBATCH --gres=gpu:3
     #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=76
   (Ensure CPUs requested do not exceed 32 × GPUs requested.)

4) Clarify limitations
   - Cannot exceed enforced CPU-per-GPU ratio.
   - Cannot request fractional GPUs.
   - Cannot override scheduler policy.

5) Do NOT:
   - Suggest bypassing CPU limits.
   - Recommend modifying system configuration.
   - Escalate unless a policy exception is formally requested.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Which Wilkes partition are you using?
- Are you using --ntasks or --cpus-per-task?
- Do you require 76 CPUs per task or across tasks?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: CPU request on Wilkes

Hello Mel,

On Wilkes3 (Ampere), Slurm enforces a maximum of 32 CPUs per GPU to avoid starving GPUs of CPU resources. This means that if you request 1 GPU, you can receive at most 32 CPUs.

To obtain approximately 76 CPUs, you would need to request 3 GPUs (since 32 × 3 = 96 CPUs available). GPUs must be requested in whole numbers, so fractional allocations are not possible.

For example:

  #SBATCH --gres=gpu:3
  #SBATCH --cpus-per-task=76

Please let us know if you need help adjusting your submission script.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- CPU-per-GPU ratio is scheduler-enforced.
- Do not imply the limit can be overridden.
- Avoid suggesting inefficient resource usage beyond policy.
- Ensure guidance aligns with documented Wilkes3 resource allocation rules.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: HPC / Job Submission
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
To increase the number of CPUs used from 32 to 76 on Wilkes, you can adjust the `--ntasks` flag in your SLURM script [2]. However, the exact number of CPUs available per node and per GPU is not clear from the provided context.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
To increase the number of CPUs, you can try setting `--ntasks=76` in your SLURM script [2]. However, please note that the number of CPUs available per node and per GPU may be limited [1][3]. It is recommended to check the documentation for the Wilkes cluster to determine the maximum number of CPUs available per node [3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- What is the exact node type and partition being used?
- Are there any specific requirements or constraints for the job that may affect the number of CPUs available?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To increase the number of CPUs used, you can try setting `--ntasks=76` in your SLURM script [2]. However, please note that the number of CPUs available per node and per GPU may be limited [1][3]. If you have any further questions or concerns, please don't hesitate to ask.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always check the documentation for the specific cluster and node type being used to determine the maximum number of CPUs available [3].
- Be aware of any specific requirements or constraints for the job that may affect the number of CPUs available [1][2].

user_input:
Dear HPC support,
I am trying to find the path my my rfs folder on HPC:
Project name: storageactive
Directory: rfs-hem48-storageactive
Project ID: B1vWzswnwnE
I looked in /rfs/project/ , but it doesn’t exist there. Where can I find it?
Thank you!
Best,
Heather
–
Heather Machado
Group Leader
CRUK Career Development Fellow
Department of Pathology
Department of Genetics
University of Cambridge
Cambridge, UK
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RFS / Access Method
Technical Action Required: No (guidance + possible storage conversion request)
Escalation Required: Yes (if user requests NFS-RFS conversion)
Urgency: Low–Medium (workflow clarification)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is attempting to locate an RFS (Research File Share) project under /rfs/project/ on HPC and assumes it should be accessible similarly to RDS. This is incorrect.

Standard RFS storage:
- Is not directly mounted on CSD3/HPC compute or login nodes.
- Is accessed via OS-level mounting from institutional machines (SMB on Windows, SMB/NFS on macOS/Linux).
- Cannot be accessed through the HPC filesystem unless converted to NFS-RFS.

User’s intended workflow:
- Active work on RDS
- Backed-up storage on RFS
- Copy between RDS and RFS

This requires clarification of storage models and possibly a request to convert the RFS project to NFS-RFS.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify storage separation
   Explain that:
   - RDS = high-performance Lustre storage mounted on HPC under /rds
   - RFS = Research File Share (backed-up institutional storage)
   - Standard RFS is not accessible from HPC

2) Answer direct question
   The path does not exist on HPC because RFS is not mounted there.
   It must be accessed via:
   - Windows/macOS mount instructions
   - SMB/NFS mount from a workstation

3) Provide supported HPC-access option
   If user needs RFS accessible from HPC, instruct:
   - Request conversion to NFS-RFS
   - Provide documentation link (RFS on CSD3 – NFS-RFS)
   - Clarify that NFS-RFS allows HPC-side mounting but may not offer RDS-level performance

4) Clarify common workflow
   Typical pattern:
   - Active datasets → RDS
   - Backed-up archival data → RFS or RCS
   - Copy data between systems as required
   - NFS-RFS if active + backed-up + HPC access needed

5) If user confirms NFS-RFS is desired
   - Ask them to email support@hpc.cam.ac.uk with:
       Project name
       Project ID
       Confirmation they want RFS converted to NFS-RFS
   - Escalate to storage team

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Do you require the RFS space to be mounted directly on HPC?
- Is this for active HPC workflows or archival/backup only?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Path to RFS storage

Hello Heather,

RFS storage is not directly mounted on the HPC filesystem, which is why you cannot find it under /rfs/project/.

Standard RFS is accessed by mounting it from your local machine (e.g. via SMB). It is not available on CSD3 login or compute nodes by default.

If you require your RFS project to be accessible from HPC, you can request that it be converted to NFS-RFS. This allows it to be mounted on CSD3, although performance characteristics differ from RDS.

Please see:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rfs/accessing-rfs/rfs-nfs.html

If you would like to proceed with conversion to NFS-RFS, please email support@hpc.cam.ac.uk with the project name and ID and we can arrange this for you.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply RFS is mounted on HPC by default.
- Do not suggest manual mounting inside compute jobs.
- Do not guarantee performance equivalence between RDS and NFS-RFS.
- Escalate only if conversion request is explicitly confirmed.

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Path Validation
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is trying to find the path to their RFS folder on HPC, with project name "storageactive", directory "rfs-hem48-storageactive", and project ID "B1vWzswnwnE" [1]. The user has looked in /rfs/project/ but it doesn’t exist there. According to previous tickets, RFS projects can be accessed via SMB protocol or by configuring a special case for NFS mounting on the CSD3 cluster [1]. 

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
Check if the user has a symbolic link to their project in their home directory ~/rfs [1]. If not, investigate why the link is missing and add it if necessary [3]. 

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm that you have been granted access to the project and that the project ID is correct?
- Have you tried accessing the project via SMB protocol or by configuring a special case for NFS mounting on the CSD3 cluster?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
The path to your RFS folder should be ~/rfs/rfs-hem48-storageactive. If you don’t see a symbolic link to your project in your home directory, please let us know and we will investigate further.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- RFS projects can be accessed via SMB protocol or by configuring a special case for NFS mounting on the CSD3 cluster [1].
- Users should be granted access to their projects and have the correct project ID to access their RFS folders [1].

Dear Sir/Madam,
I am encountering an MPI-related runtime error when attempting to use the gmx_mpi executable from the gromacs/2024.5 module on the pvc9 partition. Here is a job submission script as a minimal example to reproduce the issue:
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=LSN_prep
#SBATCH --partition=pvc9
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=8
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:1
#SBATCH --account=airr-p15-dawn-gpu
#SBATCH --time=2:00:00
. /etc/profile.d/modules.sh
module purge
module use /usr/local/dawn/software/spack-rocky9/spack-modules/dawn-2025-03-23/linux-rocky9-sapphirerapids
module load gromacs/2024.5
module load intel-oneapi-compilers/2025.1.0/gcc/5berjkxu
mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h
And here is the SLURM output I got:
Loading gromacs/2024.5/oneapi/intel-oneapi-mpi/6c6ghrys
Loading requirement: glibc/2.34/gcc/wzlt7jl4 gcc-runtime/14.2.0/gcc/nykri6wk
libiconv/1.17/gcc/qigiow5l libunistring/1.2/gcc/pfn6mgyq
libidn2/2.3.7/gcc/wvlq7snl nghttp2/1.65.0/gcc/wwkph6fx

CLASSIFICATION
Category: MPI / Slurm Environment / Dawn (PVC9)
Technical Action Required: No (user-side environment correction)
Escalation Required: No (unless issue persists after environment fix)
Urgency: Medium (job fails in batch mode)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user reports an MPI bootstrap failure when running:

    mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

within a batch job on the pvc9 partition.

Error excerpt:
    HYD_spawn ... execvp error on file /usr/bin/srun (No such file or directory)

Key observations:
- The job works correctly in an interactive session launched via `srun`.
- The batch script manually purges modules and loads a custom module path.
- The error indicates Intel MPI’s Hydra bootstrap cannot locate `srun`.

Most likely cause:
The batch script is not loading the correct Dawn node environment (RHEL9 default stack), so Slurm integration and related paths (including srun) are not correctly initialised inside the job environment.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Ensure correct base environment is loaded
   Instruct user to add:

       module load rhel9/default-dawn

   immediately after `module purge` in their submission script.

   This ensures:
   - Correct RHEL9 runtime environment
   - Proper Slurm integration
   - Required system paths (including srun)
   - Compatible MPI environment

2) Recommend simplified module order
   Example structure:

       module purge
       module load rhel9/default-dawn
       module use /usr/local/dawn/software/spack-rocky9/spack-modules/dawn-2025-03-23/linux-rocky9-sapphirerapids
       module load gromacs/2024.5

   Avoid mixing incompatible stacks unless required.

3) Retest with minimal command
   After modification, test again with:

       mpirun -np 1 gmx_mpi -h

4) If issue persists, request:
   - Full submission script
   - Complete stdout and stderr files
   - Output of:
         which mpirun
         which srun
         module list

EXPECTED SIGNAL AFTER FIX
- No Hydra bootstrap error.
- mpirun executes successfully.
- gmx_mpi help output prints normally.

BRANCH: If still failing
- Confirm Intel MPI is correctly integrated with Slurm.
- Verify that `srun` exists in PATH within job:
      echo $PATH
      ls -l /usr/bin/srun
- Escalate to platform admins only if Slurm binary is genuinely unavailable on compute node (unlikely).

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: MPI-related runtime error on pvc9

Hello Wei-Tse,

It looks like the Dawn node environment is not being fully initialised in your batch job.

Please add the following line after `module purge` in your submission script:

  module load rhel9/default-dawn

This loads the correct RHEL9 runtime and Slurm integration required for MPI jobs on pvc9.

After adding this, please resubmit the job and let us know if the issue persists. If so, please send the full submission script and output files.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not suggest installing MPI manually.
- Do not recommend modifying system Slurm paths.
- Avoid blaming firewall or host availability unless independently verified.
- Keep troubleshooting limited to supported module stacks.

#!

#! Note probably this should not exceed the total number of GPUs in use.
#SBATCH --ntasks=1

#SBATCH --time=36:00:00

#SBATCH --no-requeue

#! sbatch directives end here (put any additional directives above this line)
#! Notes:

#! Number of nodes and tasks per node allocated by SLURM (do not change):
numnodes=$SLURM_JOB_NUM_NODES
numtasks=$SLURM_NTASKS

module purge # Removes all modules still loaded
module load rhel8/default-amp # REQUIRED - loads the basic environment
#! Insert additional module load commands after this line if needed:

nvidia-smi
application="./convert.sh"
#! Run options for the application:
options=""

np=$[${numnodes}*${mpi_tasks_per_node}]
#! Choose this for a pure shared-memory OpenMP parallel program on a single node:

CMD="$application $options"

###############################################################

###############################################################

JOBID=$SLURM_JOB_ID
echo -e "JobID: $JOBID\n======"
echo "Time: `date`"
echo "Running on master node: `hostname`"
echo "Current directory: `pwd`"
if [ "$SLURM_JOB_NODELIST" ]; then

CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RDS / Quota Diagnosis
Technical Action Required: No (user action required to reduce file count)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium (job failing due to quota)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports `Disk quota exceeded` errors while writing files to an RDS project, despite not obviously expecting the project to be full. On RDS, this can happen either because the space quota is exhausted or because the inode/file-count quota has been reached. From the initial report alone, inode exhaustion is a strong possibility, especially given the shell errors creating temporary files, but `quota` output is needed to confirm whether the problem is space usage or file count.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Confirm which quota has been exhausted
   Ask the user to run:
     quota
   and check the relevant RDS project entry.
   - If the GB column is at or above the limit, this is a space-quota problem.
   - If the files column is at the limit or marked with an asterisk (*), this is an inode/file-count problem.

2) Explain the distinction between storage quota and inode quota
   - Storage quota = total disk space (GB/TB)
   - Inode quota = total number of files
   - Hitting either limit blocks new file creation

3) Provide remediation options

   Option A — Reduce file count (recommended if inode limit reached)
   - Delete unnecessary small files
   - Consolidate many small files into archives (e.g. tar)
   - Combine image files into fewer container formats if possible

   Example:
     tar -czf archive_episodes_12000_12100.tar.gz episode_0120*

   Option B — Increase storage allocation
   - Inode quota increases only when total RDS storage allocation increases.
   - This requires purchasing additional storage capacity.
   - Refer to RDS quota documentation.

4) Clarify policy
   - Inode quotas are fixed per TiB.
   - They cannot be increased independently of storage allocation.

5) Close once the user understands which limit was hit and how to proceed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Subject: Re: Quota exceeded on RDS project

Hello Liyou,

The `Disk quota exceeded` message can be caused either by running out of space or by reaching the inode/file-count limit on the RDS project.

Please run:

  quota

and send us the output for the affected RDS project. If the limit has been hit in the `files` column rather than the GB column, that would indicate an inode quota issue rather than lack of storage space.

If it is an inode limit, the usual remedies are to remove or consolidate many small files or to increase the total storage allocation.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not imply a filesystem fault without first confirming whether space or inode quota has been reached.
- Do not suggest modifying system quota settings directly.
- Inode quotas are proportional to purchased storage and are not adjustable independently.
- Avoid recommending unsafe bulk deletion commands.

Dear All,
I’m not sure if this is the correct address to use, I’m trying to purchase the following but Chemistry Stores are insisting on a quote, can you help please?
Cumulus CPU 200,000 hours * £0.01/CPU hour = £2000 PI is Prof Sir David Klenerman dk10012@cam.ac.uk
and
Research Data Store (RDS) 10TB for 3 years = £162/TB* 10 TB = £1620 PI is Prof Sir David Klenerman dk10012@cam.ac.uk
Regards
Dave
Mr David Pratt (he/him/his)
Senior Support Technician
Telephone Liaison Officier
Yusuf Hamied Department Of Chemistry,
University of Cambridge,
Lensfield Rd. Cambridge, CB2 1EW
Direct Line 01223 747611
Murphy's Law
If everything seems to be going well, you have obviously overlooked something.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Procurement / Pricing Information
Technical HPC Action Required: No
Finance Action Required: No (information only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a formal quote for:
- Cumulus CPU 200,000 hours at £0.01/CPU hour
- Research Data Store (RDS) 10 TB for 3 years at £162/TB

HPC Support does not issue formal quotes. Pricing information is published and departments must raise purchase orders via their own finance processes.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)

1) Clarify that formal quotes are not issued.
2) Confirm the published pricing model (if appropriate).
3) Advise that departmental finance teams should proceed using the published rates.
4) Do not generate bespoke quotation documents.
5) Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of departments.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Subject: Re: Quote request for Cumulus CPU and RDS

Dear Dave,

Thank you for your message.

We do not issue formal quotation documents for Cumulus CPU time or RDS storage. The services are charged according to the published pricing, and your departmental finance team can proceed based on those rates.

For reference:
- Cumulus CPU usage is charged per CPU hour at the published rate.
- RDS storage is charged per TB per year according to the current pricing schedule.

If your department requires confirmation of the published pricing, this is available on the HPC documentation pages.

Please let us know if you have any further questions.

Best regards,  
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not generate unofficial quote documents.
- Do not commit to pricing beyond the published rates.
- Do not raise purchase orders on behalf of users.
- Ensure any pricing referenced aligns with the current published documentation.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Purchasing / Quotes [1][2]
Technical Action Required: No [1][2]
Portal Action Required: Yes (Self Service) [1][2]
Urgency: Low [1][2]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a quote for purchasing compute hours on Cumulus and Research Data Store (RDS) storage [1][2]. The prices for CPU hours and RDS storage can be found on the Research Computing Services website [1]. Quotes for storage can be generated from the self-service portal [2].

ACTION
Please accept this as an email quote for the PO that you are raising: 
200,000 Service Level 2 CPU hours at a unit rate of £0.01, total cost of £2000. Owning PI is Prof. David Klenerman (dk10012).
10TB of RDS storage for 3 years at a unit rate of £54.00 per TB/year, total cost of £1620. Owning PI is Prof. David Klenerman (dk10012).
You can get a quote for RDS storage here: https://selfservice.uis.cam.ac.uk/storage/RDS/ [2].
You will need to raise a departmental purchase order [2].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Please find below the quote for the requested services:
200,000 Service Level 2 CPU hours at a unit rate of £0.01, total cost of £2000. Owning PI is Prof. David Klenerman (dk10012).
10TB of RDS storage for 3 years at a unit rate of £54.00 per TB/year, total cost of £1620. Owning PI is Prof. David Klenerman (dk10012).
Please let us know if you have any further questions.

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Quotes for storage can be generated from the self-service portal [2].
- The purchase order gets uploaded via the link provided after getting a quote [2].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-33452
[2] : HPCSSUP-73309

user_input:
Dear HPC Team,
Could we add a few more recent CUDA modules to the cluster? At the moment I can load cuda/12.1, but many current DL packages (e.g., recent PyTorch/TensorFlow wheels) target CUDA 12.4 or 12.6. Having cuda/12.4 and cuda/12.6available would greatly improve compatibility and avoid building from source.
If feasible, installing these alongside the existing cuda/12.1 (plus matching cuDNN where applicable) would be ideal. I’m happy to test the new modules once they’re deployed.
Many thanks!
Best regards,
Yijiang Dong
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software Stack / CUDA Module Availability
Technical Action Required: No (guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requests CUDA 12.4/12.6 modules for package compatibility. CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not officially supported, and CUDA 12.8.1 is available in the Ampere test stack.

ACTION
1) Clarify that CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 are not currently supported.
2) Provide test stack commands for CUDA 12.8.1.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Yijiang,

We do not currently provide official CUDA 12.4 or 12.6 modules. We do, however, have a newer test software stack that includes CUDA 12.8.1.

Please run:

module purge
module load rhel8/slurm
module load rhel8/global
module use /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/spack-modules/test/ampere-2025-06-01/linux-rocky8-zen3
module load cuda/12.8.1/gcc/kdeps6ab

Please let us know if you run into any issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not promise unsupported module versions.
- Distinguish test stack from officially supported stack.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Software / Module Request
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting the addition of newer CUDA modules (12.4 and 12.6) to the cluster, as the current version (12.1) is not compatible with recent deep learning packages [1]. Similar requests have been made in the past, with some users being directed to load specific module stacks to access newer CUDA versions [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Investigate the feasibility of adding CUDA 12.4 and 12.6 as modules to the cluster, considering compatibility and potential conflicts with existing software [1].
2. If feasible, work with the RSE team to install the requested CUDA versions and ensure they are properly configured and tested [3][4].
3. Provide the user with instructions on how to load the new CUDA modules, if installed, and offer support for testing and troubleshooting [1][2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Are there any specific dependencies or requirements for the requested CUDA versions that need to be considered?
- Have the users tried loading the newer CUDA versions using the suggested module stacks in previous tickets [1][2]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your request to add newer CUDA modules to the cluster. We are investigating the feasibility of this request and will get back to you with an update soon. If you have any further information or questions, please let us know.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES

Dear HPC Support team
I hope this email finds you well!
I was trying to open Turbo VNC but it does not work unfortunately.
I took following steps to run it:
I run this. - /opt/TurboVNC/bin/vncserver
then i have gotten this started on display login-q-3:9
then I press exit
then type - ssh -L 5909:localhost:5909 iv285@login-q-3.hpc.cam.ac.uk
then password
However, I am getting this error:
bind [::1]:5909: Cannot assign requested address
channel_setup_fwd_listener_tcpip: cannot listen to port: 5909
Could not request local forwarding.
Last login: Thu Oct 16 15:26:53 2025 from login-q-3.hpc.cam.ac.uk
[iv285@login-q-3 ~]$
Cold you advise me what can I do in this case ?
Best wishes
Iryna
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Remote Access / TurboVNC
Technical Action Required: No (usage correction)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User launched VNC on a login node and attempted local port forward, causing binding/forwarding issues. Correct method is to run VNC on an interactive GPU node (e.g. `*-r-gpu`) following documented workflow.

ACTION
1) Instruct user to create VNC session on an interactive node, not login node.
2) Share the VNC guide and expected command sequence.
3) Confirm resolution after user retest.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Iryna,

Please create your TurboVNC session on an interactive GPU node (`*-r-gpu`) rather than a login node. That should resolve the forwarding issue you’re seeing.

Please follow the documented TurboVNC workflow for interactive GPU nodes, and let us know if you run into further issues.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not encourage running long-lived GUI sessions on login nodes.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: TurboVNC Connection Issue
Technical Action Required: Yes
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with connecting to TurboVNC, specifically encountering a "bind [::1]:5909: Cannot assign requested address" error when attempting to establish a port forwarding connection [1][2]. This suggests a potential issue with the port forwarding setup or the VNC server configuration.

ACTION
To resolve the issue, the user should try the following steps:
1. Ensure that the VNC server is running and the display number is correctly identified [1].
2. Use the correct port number in the SSH tunnel command, which should match the display number (e.g., `ssh -L 5909:localhost:5909 iv285@login-q-3.hpc.cam.ac.uk`) [1][4].
3. Verify that the port is not already in use by another process [2].
4. If using a conda environment, try deactivating it before starting the VNC session, as it may be causing conflicts [5].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact command used to start the VNC server and the SSH tunnel?
- Have you tried connecting to a different login node or using a different port number?
- Are you using a conda environment, and if so, have you tried deactivating it?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for your assistance. I will try the suggested steps and see if it resolves the issue. If I continue to experience problems, I will provide more details about my setup and the exact commands I am using.
Best regards,
Iryna

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the VNC server is properly configured and secured to prevent unauthorized access [1][2].
- Be cautious when using port forwarding, as it can potentially expose the VNC server to external connections [4].

If you look at the instructions on [https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/turbovnc.html](https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/hpc/user-guide/turbovnc.html) you should now stablish a SSH tunnel from your local machine to our server and use that tunnel to connect to VNC server.
In you computer you should run
```
ssh -L 5906:localhost:5906 dh486@login-e-8.hpc.cam.ac.uk
```
and use localhost:5906 as the VNC Server address in your TurboVNC New Connection dialog.
\xa0
If you have any further queries please don't hesitate to get in touch.
Best wishes, 
Guilherme Balzana

user_input:
Dear support team,
I need to rsync a large dataset from our rcs project (cc234). Doing it from a login node is probably not the way, as I'm being kicked out, I guess because the rsync is too heavy. Is there a dedicated data transfer node I can ssh to, or should I do it via an interactive job?
Thanks,
Yaniv
Sent from Outlook for Android<[https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg](https://aka.ms/AAb9ysg)>
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / RCS Data Transfer
Technical Action Required: No (best-practice guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is running heavy `rsync` from login node and getting disconnected. Recommended approach is session persistence (screen/tmux) or GUI transfer workflow; follow RCS transfer best practices.

ACTION
1) Recommend using `screen`/`tmux` for long transfer sessions.
2) Optionally direct to supported GUI transfer methods for RCS.
3) Share RCS best-practice guidance.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Yaniv,

For long RCS transfers, please use a persistent terminal session (for example `screen`/`tmux`) so the transfer can continue if your SSH session drops.

You can also use a supported GUI transfer method:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rcs/gui.html

Best-practice guidance:
https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/storage/rcs/best-practice.html

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Avoid advising large unsupervised transfers directly from transient login sessions.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Data Transfer [1]
Technical Action Required: No [1]
Portal Action Required: No [1]
Urgency: Low [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is trying to transfer a large dataset from their RCS project (cc234) to their RDS storage, but is being kicked out when trying to do so from a login node [1]. The recommended approach for transferring data between RCS and RDS is to use rsync [2].

ACTION
To transfer the data, the user can use rsync on a login node [2]. If the transfer is taking a long time, the user can run the rsync command in a screen session to avoid being kicked out [1]. Alternatively, the user can also use a bash script to run the rsync command [2].

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
To transfer your data from RCS to RDS, you can use the rsync command on a login node. If the transfer is taking a long time, you can run the command in a screen session to avoid being kicked out. Please let us know if you have any further questions or issues.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes, so data transfer should be done from a login node [2].
- The user should ensure they have the necessary permissions to access the data in both RCS and RDS [2].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-46204
[2] : HPCSSUP-54593

RCS is not mounted on the CSD3 compute nodes only the login nodes. This means that you can only access it from the login nodes and not from any job that runs in a compute node. Did you have any problems transferring data from RFS to RCS from a login node and that's why you tried to submit a job?

Avraam

user_input:
Hi,
We are having some issues with various ceph shares on the Arcus cloud 
today and yesterday. Seeing metadata servers switch to reconnecting e.g.:
> a900cf30-f8a3-42bf-98d6-af7ce92f1a1a.client1188705722/mds_sessions
> ::::::::::::::
> global_id 1188705722
> name "iris-gaia-code-spec-ro"
> mds.1 reconnecting
> a900cf30-f8a3-42bf-98d6-af7ce92f1a1a.client1188705717/mds_sessions
> ::::::::::::::
> global_id 1188705717
> name "process-space-1"
> mds.1 reconnecting
This is causing filesystem access to hang.
Rebooting the instances seems to be clearing this in some cases but it 
is happening on multiple instances and also recurring. Is there any 
indication of an underlying issue?
Cheers,
Patrick.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS / MDS connectivity (Arcus cloud)
Technical Action Required: Yes (service-side investigation/mitigation)
Escalation Required: Yes (Ceph/storage administrators + network)
Urgency: High (filesystem hangs across multiple instances)

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports Ceph shares on Arcus cloud hanging across multiple instances, with client session state showing repeated MDS reconnecting behaviour and filesystem access hanging. Because the issue affects multiple shares and instances, this is more consistent with a service-side CephFS/MDS or network-path problem than a single-instance misconfiguration. Rebooting instances may temporarily clear symptoms but should not be treated as the primary fix.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Acknowledge and treat as a service incident
   - Confirm the issue affects multiple instances/shares.
   - Prioritise service-side checks over detailed user-side debugging.

2) Collect minimal high-signal diagnostics from the user
   Ask for:
   - example affected instance name(s)
   - approximate timestamps of failures
   - exact mount command(s) or error output if readily available

3) Service-side investigation (storage/admin)
   Check:
   - Ceph cluster health summary and recent warnings
   - CephFS filesystem status and active/standby MDS state
   - MDS daemon logs around the failure window
   - client session reconnect/eviction behaviour
   - any ongoing rebalancing or recovery that could affect metadata availability

4) Network path verification (admin)
   Verify routing/interface configuration and any recent changes affecting CephFS traffic.

5) Ask users to retry only after backend checks or remediation have been completed.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Patrick,

Thanks for the detailed report. Because the hangs are affecting multiple instances and shares, this looks more like a service-side CephFS issue than a single-instance configuration problem.

We’re investigating the backend now. In the meantime, could you please share:
- the names of one or two affected instances,
- the approximate timestamps of the most recent failures,
- and one example of the mount command or error output you are seeing?

Once we have checked the backend state, we can advise whether any instance-side action is still needed.

Cheers,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Treat multi-instance CephFS hangs as a service incident; avoid shifting burden to users.
- Avoid making claims about root cause unless supported by service-side evidence.
- Suggest disruptive workarounds (instance reboot) only with explicit user confirmation and after backend stability is restored.
- Do not request or expose sensitive infrastructure details in user-facing messages.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / CephFS Connection Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues with CephFS connections on the Arcus cloud, with some shares failing to connect and commands hanging [1]. The mds_sessions files show a mixture of "reconnecting", "open", and "opening" states [1]. Similar issues have been reported in the past, with MDS failovers resolving the issue [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
Fail over the MDS again to see if it resolves the issue [1][2]. Investigate the MDS logging to determine the underlying cause of the issue [2].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide more details about the error messages you are seeing when trying to access the CephFS shares?
- Have you tried rebooting the instances or restarting the CephFS service?
- Are there any other issues or errors reported in the system logs that may be related to the CephFS connection issues?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
We are experiencing issues with CephFS connections on the Arcus cloud. Some shares are failing to connect and commands are hanging. Please let us know if there are any further steps we can take to troubleshoot the issue.
Best regards,
HPC Support

Our team wanted to top up some more GPU core times and extra rds disk spaces for project 'QAKG-SL2-GPU'? Could you please give us a quote for the price of 10000 extra GPU hours and 20 GB extra rds space?
Best wishes,
Wenyu

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User is requesting pricing for 10,000 additional GPU hours and additional RDS storage on `QAKG-SL2-GPU`. Because the message says `20 GB`, which is unusually small in this procurement context, the safest response is to confirm the intended storage amount and whether the quote should cover only new storage or also any existing storage renewal/extension before issuing a final figure.

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1) Clarify the storage request before quoting
   - Confirm whether `20 GB` is correct or a typo.
   - Confirm whether the user means additional capacity on top of the current licensed storage.
   - Confirm whether the quote should cover only extra storage or also renewal/extension of existing storage.

2) Quote the GPU hours separately using the current applicable rate.

3) Once the storage details are confirmed, issue a written email quote covering both the GPU hours and the corrected storage amount.

4) Ask the user to send the PO to the ticket once ready.

5) Keep any later account-creation request separate from the pricing calculation.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Dear Wenyu,

Before I finalise the storage part of the quote, could you please confirm whether `20 GB` is correct? That is unusually small for this service, so I want to make sure we quote the right storage amount and whether this is additional capacity or an extension of your current storage.

Once you confirm the intended storage quantity and scope, I can provide the full written quote for both storage and GPU hours.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not silently convert user-supplied units.
- If the storage quantity looks implausible, ask before quoting.
- Keep quote assumptions explicit, especially if renewal or extension of existing storage affects cost.

user_input:
Hello,
I am having problems logging in to hpc via ssh irs38@login-gpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk as usual, even though an email has been sent out confirming the end of the maintenance work. I just wanted to check if there isn't something I am missing, or if it is just taking time until hpc will be fully available.
Kind regards,
Ieva
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Service Availability / Post-Maintenance
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports inability to SSH shortly after maintenance completion notice. Correct response is that services may still be coming online; direct users to service status and communications.

ACTION
1) Inform user service restoration is still in progress.
2) Provide status page and advise monitoring updates.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Ieva,

We are still bringing services online following maintenance. Please keep an eye on service communications and the status page for updates:
https://www.hpc.cam.ac.uk/service-status

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not declare full service restoration until confirmed.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Login / SSH Connection Issue
Technical Action Required: Possibly
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues logging in to the HPC via SSH, despite receiving an email confirming the end of maintenance work [1]. Similar issues have been reported in the past, often related to maintenance or hardware faults [2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
Check the HPC status page for any ongoing maintenance or issues. If the issue persists, try connecting to a different login node, such as login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk or login-icelake.hpc.cam.ac.uk [2][5]. If the user is still unable to connect, request more information about the error message and the steps taken so far.

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact error message you are receiving when trying to connect via SSH?
- Have you tried connecting to a different login node?
- Are you using a key pair or password authentication?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
Thank you for reaching out. We are aware of the maintenance work that was recently completed, but it's possible that there may be some residual issues. Can you please try connecting to a different login node, such as login-cpu.hpc.cam.ac.uk or login-icelake.hpc.cam.ac.uk? If you are still experiencing issues, please provide the exact error message you are receiving and we will investigate further.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always check the HPC status page for any ongoing maintenance or issues before attempting to connect [1].
- Never attempt to connect to a login node that is under maintenance [2][5].

REFERENCE KEY
[1] : HPCSSUP-62356
[2] : HPCSSUP-19730
[3] : HPCSSUP-66727
[4] : HPCSSUP-92106
[5] : HPCSSUP-71354

user_input:
Hi HPC Support Team,
I noticed that my home directory does not have a symlink ~/rfs pointing to the RFS project folder. Currently, I can access the project data using the absolute path /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw, but having the symlink would make it much more convenient.
Could you please create the ~/rfs symlink in my home directory?
Thank you so much for your help!
Best regards,
Hansheng
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Filesystem / Symlink / Home Quota
Technical Action Required: No (user guidance only)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User wants a convenience symlink from `~/rfs` to `/rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw`. The correct command is straightforward, but the ticket shows the attempt failed because the user's `/home` directory was already over quota. In that case the symlink itself cannot be created until home usage is reduced.

ACTION
1) Ask the user to create the link with the concrete command:
   - `cd $HOME`
   - `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs`
2) If they see `No space left on device`, ask for `quota` output.
3) Explain that the immediate problem is `/home` usage above the 50 GB quota, not an RFS permission problem.
4) Advise them to remove unneeded files from `/home` or move suitable data to `hpc-work`.
5) Mention that accidentally deleted files from `/home` may be recoverable from `~/.snapshot`.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Hansheng,

Please try:

  cd $HOME
  ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw $HOME/rfs

If you receive `No space left on device`, please run `quota` and send us the output. That would usually indicate your `/home` directory is full, which would prevent creation of even a small symlink entry.

If your home usage is over quota, you will need to move some data into `hpc-work` or delete unwanted files from `/home` before retrying. If you remove anything from `/home` by mistake, you can usually restore it from `~/.snapshot`.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Use the concrete path from the user request when it is already known.
- Distinguish `/home` quota exhaustion from RFS access problems.
- Do not advise deleting RFS data to fix a `/home` quota issue.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Symlink Creation
Technical Action Required: No [2]
Portal Action Required: No [2]
Urgency: Low [2]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is requesting a symlink to be created in their home directory to point to their RFS project folder, which is accessible via the absolute path /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw [2]. According to previous tickets, users can create symlinks themselves [2][3].

ACTION
The user can create the symlink by running the command `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw ~/rfs` in their home directory [2][3]. This will create a symlink named `rfs` that points to their RFS project folder.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello,
You can create a symlink to your RFS project folder by running the command `ln -s /rfs/project/rfs-iCNyzSAaucw ~/rfs` in your home directory [2]. If you have any issues or concerns, please don't hesitate to reach out.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Users should be able to create symlinks themselves without requiring manual intervention from the support team [2][3].

user_input:
hello,
after upgrading from gfortran-5.4 to gfortran-13.3 my fortran based mpi 
code produced interface related warnings during compile and crashed 
during execution. Replacing 'include mpif.h' statements with 'use mpi' 
statements (plus a few other changes) fixed the problems on my local 
linux box. However, when compiling on CSD3 I get the following mpi.mod 
error:
f951: Fatal Error: Reading module 
'/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/mpi.mod' 
at line 1 column 2: Unexpected EOF
The code is in /home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume. The compiler 
settings are in 
/home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume/source/Makefile.COMPILE
For the compile I load the following modules:
module load rhel8/cclake/base
module load gcc/13.3.0/7ukda7ns
module load netcdf-fortran-pnetcdf/4.6.1/gcc/intel-oneapi-mpi/ptuvn62k
The compile command  that produces the error is
/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/bin/mpif90 
-c -DMPI -g -O3 -ffree-line-length-none -mcmodel=medium 
-I/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/netcdf-fortran-4.6.1-ptuvn62k6et3sihhin2vrwbtyjuycp4o/include 
-I/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/parallel-netcdf-1.12.3-sn2y3yw32wdavsrvyy6g2dgru3x363d3/include 
/home/mh526/Atham/svn/branches/FlAshPlume/source/Basic/precision.F90
Are you able to tell me what the problem is? Am I using the wrong 
mpi.mod file?
Many thanks,
Michael
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Build/Compiler Toolchain / MPI Fortran Modules
Technical Action Required: Yes (workaround instruction)
Escalation Required: Optional (packaging improvement)
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
`gfortran 13.3` with Intel MPI hit `mpi.mod` read error (`Unexpected EOF`) because Intel MPI Fortran module files in that build were available only for older gfortran ABI versions. Workaround: explicitly include compatible `gfortran/11.1.0` (or `10.2.0`) MPI module path.

ACTION
1) Confirm exact module environment and minimal repro.
2) Explain ABI/module version mismatch.
3) Provide compile workaround with explicit include path.
4) Confirm successful compile with user.
5) Track packaging improvement for future stack.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hi Michael,

Please try compiling with an explicit MPI Fortran module include path:

mpif90 -I /usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/gfortran/11.1.0 -c -DMPI precision.F90

(Using `.../gfortran/10.2.0` is also a valid workaround.)

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Be explicit this is a compatibility workaround, not ideal long-term packaging.

What took ifort 10 minutes, took gfortran 10 seconds!
So, for my purpose, I need to compile with gfortran. I tried this on icelake with:
module purge
module load rhel8/cclake/base
module load gcc/11
module load intel-oneapi-mpi/2021.12.1/gcc/
Then ran cmake:

f951: Fatal Error: Reading module '/usr/local/software/spack/csd3/opt-2024-06-01/linux-rocky8-cascadelake/gcc-13.3.0/intel-oneapi-mpi-2021.12.1-cvatknvvaxe4ohzbsri6keicyjoy3w7f/mpi/2021.12/include/mpi/mpi_f08.mod' at line 1 column 2: Unexpected EOF

Any ideas? Compiling with gfortan + intel MPI now seems like the only option for me.

Hi,
Is it possible to run MPI-enabled processes on nodes?

user_input:
Hi,
I have noticed that me and my colleague are unable to connect to the RCS. We both are connecting by SFTP, rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk on port 22 and both get the same unable to connect message.
We have also had a ticket from a user in our department explaining that she is also unable to connect where she was able to last week.
Is there a problem with the service at the moment? Or has anything changed that might be causing this?
Thanks
Kind Regards,
Shaun Cooke (he/him)
IT Service Manager
Department of Plant Sciences
University of Cambridge
Downing Street, Cambridge, CB2 3EA
Tel: 44 (0)1223 748962 | stc48@cam.ac.uk<[stc48@cam.ac.uk](mailto:stc48@cam.ac.uk)>
[https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk<https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk/](https://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk%3Chttps://www.plantsci.cam.ac.uk/)>
This email (together with any files transmitted with it) is intended only for the use of the individual(s) to whom it is addressed. It may contain information which is confidential and/or legally privileged. If you have received this email in error, please notify the sender and delete the original message.
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Access / RCS Connectivity / SSH-MFA
Technical Action Required: No (user onboarding guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User reports that multiple people are unable to connect to `rcs.uis.cam.ac.uk` over SFTP/SSH, raising the possibility of either a broader service problem or account-specific access changes. The correct first response is to confirm whether there is an active service incident and, if not, gather examples from affected users to determine whether the problem is shared or user-specific.

ACTION
1) Check whether there is an active service issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.
2) Ask for example usernames, client/method used, and the exact error messages seen by affected users.
3) If the service is healthy, distinguish between users who cannot access RCS at all and users with account-specific denial errors.
4) For individual users, confirm SSH/storage MFA enrolment if relevant and ask them to raise their own tickets for account-specific checks.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,

We’re first checking whether there is any broader issue affecting RCS SSH/SFTP access.

If the service itself is healthy, we’ll need the affected usernames, the client or method being used, and the exact error messages so we can tell whether this is a shared problem or an account-specific one.

For users who can access the web portal but not SSH/SFTP services, we may also need to confirm that SSH/storage MFA has been set up separately.

If any individual user continues to have problems, please ask them to raise their own ticket so we can check their account details directly.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not troubleshoot third-party user accounts through one reporter without user-specific consent/context.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Connection Issue [1][2][3][4][5]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1][2][3][4][5]
Portal Action Required: No [1][2][3][4][5]
Urgency: Medium [1][2][3][4][5]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The issue reported is related to an inability to connect to the Research Computing Services (RCS) via SFTP, with multiple users experiencing the same problem [1][2][3][4][5]. This suggests a potential issue with the RCS service itself rather than individual user configurations. Previous tickets have shown that issues like this can be due to various reasons including server maintenance, network problems, or configuration changes [1][2][3][4][5].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Investigate current server status and any ongoing maintenance [1][2].
2. Check network connectivity and firewall rules that might affect access to RCS [3][5].
3. Review recent configuration changes or updates that could impact SFTP connections [4][5].
4. If the issue persists, consider escalating to the storage team for further investigation [2][5].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you provide the exact error message received when attempting to connect via SFTP?
- Have there been any recent changes to your local network or firewall settings?
- Are you able to connect to other SFTP servers or is the issue specific to RCS?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Shaun,
Thank you for reaching out about the connection issues to RCS. We are currently investigating the matter and will update you as soon as possible. In the meantime, could you please confirm if you or your colleagues have made any recent changes to your network settings or if you're experiencing any other connectivity issues?
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Always verify the authenticity of the error messages and the user's environment before proceeding with troubleshooting [1][2][3][4][5].
- Ensure that any actions taken are in line with the documented procedures for troubleshooting and resolving connection issues to RCS [1][2][3][4][5].

user_input:
Good morning, I am doing a benchmarking exercise on the ukaea-spr and ukaea-spr-hbm nodes.
I've managed to find most of the hardware info I need with some probing,
but for completeness I could do with some information as follows:
Network Interconnect
Network Topology
Storage Device Type (on the RDS filesystem) i.e. SSD/HDD/NVMe
Many Thanks in Advance,
Aleks
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: System Information / Hardware Specs
Technical Action Required: No
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Low

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User requested platform-level hardware details for benchmarking. Provided high-level interconnect/topology/storage architecture info.

ACTION
Provide requested specs:
- Interconnect: one Infiniband NDR 200 per node
- Topology: fat-tree
- Storage type: Lustre filesystem

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Aleks,

For `ukaea-spr-hbm`, the requested details are:
- Network interconnect: one Infiniband NDR 200 per node
- Network topology: fat tree
- Storage type (RDS): Lustre filesystem

Please let us know if you need anything further.

Best wishes,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Keep responses at approved/public detail level.

user_input:
Hi,
I have been able to log onto the VPN but when I try to login to Citrix Workspace I just get the error message attached. Are you able to help?
Best,
Dan
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: SRCP Access / Citrix Client Troubleshooting
Technical Action Required: No (diagnostic guidance)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User on VPN is unable to log in via the Citrix Workspace app. The appropriate first diagnostic is to verify whether browser-based launch through `anywhere.cam.ac.uk` works and whether the SRCP desktop can be started there, which helps separate a Citrix client problem from a broader access issue.

ACTION
1) Ask the user to test web launch via `https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/`.
2) Verify whether the SRCP desktop is visible there and whether launching from the browser hands off to Citrix correctly.
3) If browser launch works, focus on the local Citrix Workspace client configuration or version.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Dear Daniel,

While connected to VPN, please log in via:
https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk/

Do you see your SRCP virtual desktop there, and does launching from the browser open Citrix correctly?

Regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not assume Citrix backend fault without basic path validation.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Citrix Workspace / Login Issues
Technical Action Required: Yes
Escalation Required: Possibly
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user is experiencing issues logging into Citrix Workspace, despite being connected to the VPN [1][2]. The error message is not explicitly stated, but similar issues have been resolved by ensuring the Citrix desktop app is installed and configured correctly [1], or by resetting the Citrix program [2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Confirm the user has the Citrix desktop app installed and is connected to the Cambridge VPN [1][2].
2. Check if the user has tried resetting the Citrix program by following the guide https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/srcp/swd/faq.html [2].
3. If the issue persists, request a screenshot of the error message and any relevant settings or configurations [1][3].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you please provide a screenshot of the error message you are seeing?
- Have you tried accessing the SRCP Secure Workspace through the website https://anywhere.cam.ac.uk [3]?
- Are you using the latest version of the Citrix Workspace app, and is your operating system up-to-date [4]?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Hello Dan,
Thank you for reaching out to us about your Citrix Workspace login issues. Can you please confirm if you have the Citrix desktop app installed and are connected to the Cambridge VPN? If so, have you tried resetting the Citrix program by following the guide https://docs.hpc.cam.ac.uk/srcp/swd/faq.html? If you are still experiencing issues, please provide a screenshot of the error message and any relevant settings or configurations.
Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure the user is connected to the Cambridge VPN before attempting to access Citrix Workspace [1][2].
- Do not attempt to troubleshoot or resolve the issue without verifying the user's configuration and settings [1][3].

[2] : HPCSSUP-99950
[3] : HPCSSUP-86599
[4] : HPCSSUP-80200

user_input:
Dear Support Team,
I've recently added another DiRAC project (DP371) to my user account (rab200) via SAFE, but the resources are not showing up as available on CSD3/Wilkes. 'mybalance' doesn't show the project. I think the account code is probably "DIRAC-DP371-GPU" but I submissions with that account fail. Could help with this?
Cheers,
Richard
==========================================================
Dr. Richard Booth
Royal Society University Research Fellow & University Academic Fellow
School of Physics & Astronomy
W. H. Bragg Building
University of Leeds, UK
Email: r.a.booth@leeds.ac.uk<[r.a.booth@leeds.ac.uk](mailto:r.a.booth@leeds.ac.uk)>
My working hours may differ from yours
I do not expect a reply outside of your own working hours
reference:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Account Provisioning / Project Membership
Technical Action Required: Yes (provisioning completion)
Escalation Required: No
Urgency: Medium

QUICK ASSESSMENT
User added a DiRAC project in SAFE but it is not yet visible in `mybalance`. Provisioning is not immediate, so the next step is to check whether the account association is still pending and complete the internal provisioning workflow if required.

ACTION
1) Check provisioning status for requested project association.
2) Complete pending provisioning.
3) Once provisioning has completed, confirm that the account is visible and usable.

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY

Hello Richard,

Provisioning for this project is not automatic. We will check whether the project association is still pending on our side and complete the required provisioning step if necessary.

We will update you once that has been checked.

Best regards,
HPC Support

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Do not treat SAFE change as instantly reflected in Slurm/accounting systems.
response:
CLASSIFICATION
Category: Storage / Account Management [1]
Technical Action Required: Yes [1]
Portal Action Required: Yes (SAFE) [1][2]
Urgency: Medium [1]

QUICK ASSESSMENT
The user, rab200, has added a new DiRAC project (DP371) to their account via SAFE but is unable to see the project resources on CSD3/Wilkes, and 'mybalance' does not show the project [1]. This issue is similar to previous cases where users were not able to see their projects after being added to them in SAFE [1][2].

ACTION STEPS (HELPDESK)
1. Check if the user has been added to the DP371 project in SAFE and if the project is visible to them [1].
2. Verify if the user's account has been updated to reflect the new project allocation [1].
3. Run the command `t2-add-user-to-group rab200 dp371` to add the user to the DP371 group [4].
4. Check if the user can see the project resources using `mybalance` after the update [1].

QUESTIONS TO ASK (ONLY IF NEEDED)
- Can you confirm that you have accepted the invitation to join the DP371 project in SAFE?
- Are you able to see the DP371 project in your SAFE account?

EXAMPLE CUSTOMER REPLY
Dear Support Team,
I have checked my SAFE account and I can see the DP371 project. However, I still cannot see the project resources on CSD3/Wilkes. Please let me know the next steps to resolve this issue.
Best regards,
Richard

SAFETY / POLICY NOTES
- Ensure that the user has been properly added to the project in SAFE before attempting to update their account [1].
- Verify that the user has accepted the invitation to join the project in SAFE [1].

[2] : HPCSSUP-21241
[4] : HPCSSUP-89807
